commands.review_summary:
The PR suppresses leading cron `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED` control diagnostics from direct cron delivery while adding token-helper and cron-delivery regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +59. Total +78 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main does not suppress `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED` before `dispatchCronDelivery` falls back to outbound cron delivery, so a leading diagnostic can be treated as deliverable text. I did not run a real Matrix cron job in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the message-suppression contract, not an automatable code repair.

target.title: fix(cron): suppress control diagnostics in delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat leading cron `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED` diagnostics as non-deliverable control replies.
- Suppress that diagnostic before direct Matrix/channel delivery while preserving ordinary explanatory text that merely mentions the token.
- Keep `NO_REQUIRED_TOOL_AVAILABLE` deliverable for now; the helper test explicitly verifies it is not suppressed.
- Add focused regression coverage for cron text delivery and helper-token matching.

## Real behavior proof
- Real trigger confirmed from a local OpenClaw setup: an enabled isolated cron with Matrix `announce` delivery instructs the spawned agent to emit `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED` when no shell/exec subprocess is actually started. Before this change, that raw control diagnostic was treated as successful user-facing cron output and could be posted to Matrix.
- After the change, the actual cron delivery path (`dispatchCronDelivery` -> `normalizeSilentReplyText` -> outbound delivery) receives `NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED: missing shell/exec/command-runner execution surface`, marks the cron delivery as attempted-but-not-delivered, does not call the outbound Matrix/channel delivery function, and does not enqueue a normal main-summary post.
- Negative behavior proof: `NO_REQUIRED_TOOL_AVAILABLE for missing non-shell tool routing` now returns `false` from `isControlDiagnosticReplyText`, so it remains deliverable and is not part of this PR's suppression behavior.
- Negative behavior proof: substantive text such as `The prior cron emitted NO_SUBPROCESS_STARTED because it lacked an exec surface.` still delivers, so the suppression is limited to the leading control-token diagnostic.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` — 70 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts` — 30 passed
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm -s lint:core`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm -s build:strict-smoke`
